images.alt: True Blue hole-by-hole aerial photo tour featuring all 18 holes
images.caption: The official True Blue 'NEW Aerial Photography' hole-by-hole gallery — drone shots of all 18 holes commissioned by the club, the cleanest single source of Strantz's bold routing.
images.attribution: Courtesy of True Blue Golf Club / Mystic Golf
images.rights_note: Official club marketing imagery. Reach out to True Blue (1-888-483-6800) or marketing@mysticgolf.com for editorial-use permission. Aerial photographer Jim Maggio is credited on related galleries.

images.alt: Fall aerial photo tour of True Blue's home stretch — holes 13 through 18
images.caption: Drone tour of the closing six holes in autumn light, anchored by the iconic blue-roofed clubhouse on 18 — exactly the editorial register the book is reaching for.
images.attribution: True Blue Golf Club drone team (Play Golf Myrtle Beach / Jim Maggio aerial network)
images.rights_note: Posted Feb 2025 by True Blue's marketing. Editorial reuse requires permission from the club.

images.alt: The opening par 5 at True Blue — 624 yards, the hardest hole on the course
images.caption: Hole 1: Strantz throws you straight into the fire. Dogleg-left par 5, waste bunkers running the full left side, creek fronting an elevated green. The course's signature 'no warm-up' opener.
images.source: True Blue Golf Club (official Hole Spotlight)
images.attribution: Courtesy of True Blue Golf Club
images.rights_note: Official club content. Editorial-use permission via True Blue marketing.

images.alt: Hole 6 'Double Vision' — True Blue's dual-green par 4
images.caption: Hole 6, 'Double Vision / Double Trouble' — Strantz built two greens used in rotation. The featured image shows the forced-carry tee shot through a pine corridor over Lowcountry wetlands.
images.attribution: Courtesy of True Blue Golf Club
images.rights_note: Official club content. LINKS Magazine's 'Top 10 Dual Greens in the U.S.' feature also covers this hole if a second source is desired.

images.alt: Gallery of True Blue's five par 3s — holes 3, 7, 11, 14, and 16
images.caption: Aerial and ground portraits of the five Strantz par 3s, with hole 14's elevated tee and hole 16's diamond green over water as the centerpieces. Photography credited to Play Golf Myrtle Beach and aerial photographer Jim Maggio.
images.attribution: Play Golf Myrtle Beach / Jim Maggio (aerial)
images.rights_note: Posted Sept 2025. Credits on the page explicitly name Play Golf Myrtle Beach and Jim Maggio; contact True Blue marketing to clear individual images.

images.alt: True Blue hole 14 — par 3 with elevated tee nearly 50 feet above the green
images.caption: Hole 14: a downhill par 3 with an hourglass green guarded by one of the biggest bunkers on the property. The tee shot drops dramatically — Strantz at his most theatrical.
images.attribution: Caledonia Golf Vacations / Mystic Golf marketing
images.rights_note: Same management as True Blue. Editorial-use permission via the Caledonia/True Blue marketing team.

images.alt: True Blue hole 16 — 208-yard par 3 with diamond green guarded by water right and a vast bunker left
images.caption: Hole 16: the visual highlight of the course. A diamond-shaped green floating above sandy banks that dissolve into a pond. Hugh Royer III called it 'a great par three' — Strantz painted on canvas.
images.source: True Blue Golf Club (official Hole Spotlight)
images.attribution: Courtesy of True Blue Golf Club / Farish Media 'Links Illustrated'
images.rights_note: Official club content with embedded Farish Media video stills. Editorial reuse via club marketing.

images.alt: Aerial drone tour of True Blue's par-4 18th, captured for 'Breaking Par with Charlie Rymer'
images.caption: The iconic closer — water hugging the entire left side, the blue-tin clubhouse roof anchoring the right, and a 50-yard-deep green sloping toward the water. Filmed in early-morning light for ESPN's 'Breaking Par with Charlie Rymer.'
images.attribution: True Blue Golf Club / Breaking Par with Charlie Rymer drone footage
images.rights_note: Posted Sept 2024. Permission needed from True Blue marketing and Rymer production team for stills.

images.alt: GolfPass photo gallery — True Blue Plantation, Pawleys Island, S.C.
images.caption: The GolfPass / Golf Advisor curated gallery — 11 frames including 'The beautiful 18th hole showcases the True Blue Plantation clubhouse' (Jason Scott Deegan/Golf Advisor) and a wide approach shot on 18 ('Courtesy of True Blue Plantation').
images.attribution: Jason Scott Deegan / Golf Advisor; some frames 'Courtesy of True Blue Plantation'
images.rights_note: Mixed rights — NBC Sports (GolfPass) and True Blue. Contact GolfPass editorial for editorial-use licensing on Deegan's frames.

images.alt: True Blue 18th approach with the blue-roofed clubhouse in view
images.caption: Ground-level frame: 'The clubhouse is in view on 18 at True Blue Golf Plantation.' Captures the famed blue-tin roof from the fairway — the editorial money shot for the clubhouse spread.
images.rights_note: NBC Sports / GolfPass editorial photography. Licensing via GolfPass.

images.alt: Complete hole-by-hole aerial photo tour of True Blue
images.caption: Myrtle Beach Golf Trips' standalone aerial photo tour — 18 drone shots with hole-by-hole captions. Strong alternative or supplement to the official photo tour.
images.attribution: Myrtle Beach Golf Trips staff (Play Golf Myrtle Beach network)
images.rights_note: Part of the Play Golf Myrtle Beach marketing umbrella. Editorial use available with credit and permission.

images.alt: Hammock Coast Golf Trail True Blue feature — clubhouse, hole 3, hole 16, hole 18 photography
images.caption: Hammock Coast (the Pawleys Island/Georgetown tourism board) carries a polished spread including the True Blue Clubhouse exterior, hole 3, hole 16, and hole 18 — useful for landscape/atmospheric frames.
images.attribution: Hammock Coast SC (Georgetown County tourism) / True Blue Golf Club
images.rights_note: Tourism-board marketing imagery; typically licensable for editorial coverage with credit.

images.alt: True Blue Golf Club official scorecard and yardage diagram
images.caption: Hole-by-hole yardage matrix and scorecard layout — the closest publicly available diagram to a yardage book. Downloadable PDF version at /wp-content/uploads/2021/11/True-Blue-Scorecard.pdf.
images.attribution: Courtesy of True Blue Golf Club
images.rights_note: Official club document. Reuse for editorial coverage of the course is standard with credit.

research_notes: ENVIRONMENT CONSTRAINT: The cowork egress allowlist blocks direct fetches of truebluegolf.com, golfpass.com, myrtlebeachgolftrips.com, hammockcoastgolftrail.com, and caledoniagolfvacations.com. WebSearch and Exa's web_fetch return clean markdown but strip out attributes, so no individual CDN URLs could be confirmed. All 13 entries in this manifest therefore use the verified gallery/source page URL rather than a direct image URL — every page was confirmed to exist and to contain the described imagery via Exa's content extraction. VERIFIED SOURCES (each page was fetched and content inspected): truebluegolf.com photo tour, the official hole-by-hole aerial gallery, hole spotlights for 1, 6, 14, 16, and 18, the 'Bold & Beautiful Par 3s' gallery (credited to Play Golf Myrtle Beach and aerial photographer Jim Maggio), GolfPass's True Blue Plantation gallery (frames credited to Jason Scott Deegan and Ian Guerin), the Myrtle Beach Golf Trips aerial photo tour, and the Hammock Coast tourism board page. KEY ATTRIBUTION NOTES: Jim Maggio is the named aerial photographer credited on the par 3s gallery and is the dominant drone source for the property. Jason Scott Deegan and Ian Guerin (GolfAdvisor / GolfPass) are the most-cited ground-level editorial photographers. The 'Breaking Par with Charlie Rymer' production captured the most cinematic 18th-hole footage. True Blue is operated under the Mystic Golf umbrella alongside Caledonia, so all editorial permission requests route through the same marketing team. GAPS / NEXT STEPS: (1) To get direct image URLs, run a follow-up pass on a machine that can fetch the source pages directly and extract from the HTML — the simplest path is to open the photo-tour page in DevTools and pull the gallery slider's image array. (2) No publicly available yardage book exists for True Blue beyond the scorecard PDF; the closest 'flyover' substitute is the truebluegolf.com/hole-by-hole-flyovers/ video page. (3) Strantz architecture appreciation sites (Fried Egg, GolfWRX) cover the course but typically embed images they don't own — for editorial reuse, route back to the original source (True Blue, GolfPass, or Jim Maggio). PRIORITY GUIDANCE: P1 entries are the strongest editorial hero candidates (official aerial gallery, hole 1, hole 6 double-green, par-3 gallery, hole 16, hole 18 finisher, GolfPass clubhouse frames). P2 are strong supplements (hole 14 via Caledonia Vacations, MBGT aerial tour, Hammock Coast spread). P3 is the scorecard/yardage diagram for the design-detail spread.
